audit_initialization.critical_requirement:
When a user requests a "full audit", "comprehensive audit", or "prepare for audit", you MUST start with Phase 1: Planning and Preparation BEFORE any analysis work.
DO NOT skip directly to class discovery or property analysis. DO NOT assume the user wants to skip planning.
The planning phase is MANDATORY and sets up the entire audit for success.

step.details:
ALWAYS start by reading .bob/mcp.json to extract: - Object Store name from mcpServers.content-repository.env.OBJECT_STORE - This name will be used for the audit folder structure
If the Object Store name is not found, ask the user to provide it.

Ask the user to confirm what type of audit they want: - Full comprehensive audit (all 7 phases) - Focused audit (specific areas only) - Quick assessment (high-level overview)
For "full audit" requests, proceed with all phases. For focused audits, clarify which areas to analyze.

step.action: Create Audit Folder Structure Using Python Script
step.details:
Use the init_audit.py script to create the standardized folder structure:
Command: python tools/init_audit.py
The script will: - Read Object Store name from .bob/mcp.json - Create audits/[ObjectStore]_[DATE]/ hierarchy - Generate reports/, data/, analysis/, recommendations/ folders - Create audit_metadata.json with initialization info - Create README.md with folder structure documentation
This ensures consistent, well-documented audit structure.
Alternative: If script fails, fall back to manual creation: execute_command: mkdir -p audits/[OS]_[DATE]/{reports,data,analysis,recommendations}
step.importance: CRITICAL - Must be done before any analysis

step.details:
Create Audit_Plan.md in the audit folder root with: - Audit overview and objectives - Scope definition - All 7 phases with tasks - Expected deliverables - Timeline estimates
This serves as the roadmap for the entire audit.

Create a todo list with all major audit tasks: - [ ] Complete planning and setup - [ ] Discover and analyze document classes - [ ] Analyze property usage - [ ] Assess folder structure - [ ] Analyze document types - [ ] Document integrations - [ ] Generate reports and recommendations
Update this list as you progress through the audit.

Test MCP server connection by calling list_root_classes. This confirms the repository is accessible before proceeding.
If connection fails, report the error and stop.

pattern.response: Execute complete initialization sequence, then proceed with all 7 phases
Execute initialization sequence, create plan, then wait for user confirmation to proceed
Execute initialization sequence, but focus analysis on specified area only
Execute initialization sequence with simplified plan, focus on high-level findings

mistake.error: Jumping directly to class discovery without planning
mistake.consequence: Disorganized outputs, missing context, incomplete analysis

mistake.error: Creating audit files in wrong location (e.g., audits/ root instead of audits/OS_DATE/)
mistake.consequence: Files scattered, hard to manage multiple audits

mistake.error: Not reading MCP config to get Object Store name
mistake.consequence: Using hardcoded or incorrect Object Store names
Files created in wrong locations, missing organization
mistake.correction: Create complete folder structure in step 3

success_criteria.criterion: Object Store name extracted from MCP config
User has confirmed scope and is ready to proceed

example_initialization.scenario: User says "Prepare for a full audit"
example_initialization.correct_sequence: 1. Read .bob/mcp.json → Extract "OS2" as Object Store name 2. Ask user to confirm full audit scope 3. Execute: python tools/init_audit.py → Creates audits/OS2_19-05/ with complete folder structure → Generates audit_metadata.json and README.md 4. Create Audit_Plan.md with all 7 phases in the audit folder 5. Initialize todo list with major tasks 6. Test MCP connection with list_root_classes 7. Inform user that planning is complete and ready to begin Phase 2
example_initialization.output_example: ✓ Created: audits/OS2_19-05/ ✓ Created: audits/OS2_19-05/reports/ ✓ Created: audits/OS2_19-05/data/ ✓ Created: audits/OS2_19-05/analysis/ ✓ Created: audits/OS2_19-05/recommendations/ ✓ Created: audits/OS2_19-05/audit_metadata.json ✓ Created: audits/OS2_19-05/README.md
